input.title: School Management System
input.description: School Management System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Angular and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with ASP.NET Core, SQL Server for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Student Enrollment
requirements: Online admission form with personal and academic details
Document upload for transcripts and ID

FR-03.title: Attendance Tracking
requirements: Attendance reports by student and class

FR-04.title: Timetable Management
requirements: Create weekly timetable for each class
Assign subjects and teachers to time slots
Timetable visible to students and parents

FR-05.title: Exam and Grade Management
requirements: Create exams with subjects, dates, and marks

FR-07.title: Parent Portal
requirements: Parents view child's attendance and grades

FR-09.title: Library Management
requirements: Catalog books with ISBN, title, author
Student book borrowing and return tracking

FR-11.title: Announcement and Events
requirements: Admin publishes announcements to all or specific classes

FR-12.title: Admin Dashboard and Reports
requirements: Overview of total students, teachers, and revenue

FR-01.title: Design Student Enrollment UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for student enrollment. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for student enrollment is complete and reviewable.
The student enrollment design is responsive and accessible.
The design for student enrollment aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Student Enrollment
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for student enrollment. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Online admission form with personal and academic details; Document upload for transcripts and ID; Enrollment fee payment.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required student enrollment user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for student enrollment are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for student enrollment is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Student Enrollment
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for student enrollment. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Online admission form with personal and academic details; Document upload for transcripts and ID; Enrollment fee payment.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for student enrollment is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for student enrollment enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for student enrollment is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Student Enrollment
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for student enrollment. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for student enrollment supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for student enrollment are defined correctly.
Schema changes for student enrollment can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Integrate Student Enrollment with External Services
FR-01.description: Integrate student enrollment with the external services or providers referenced by the SRS. Implement secure service connections, payload validation, error handling, and recovery behavior for any third-party dependency involved in the workflow.
acceptance_criteria: External or cross-system integrations required by student enrollment exchange the expected payloads successfully.
Retry, timeout, and failure handling for student enrollment integrations are implemented and observable.
Credentials, tokens, and outbound calls used by student enrollment are configured and secured correctly.

FR-01.title: Testing Student Enrollment Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for student enrollment. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for student enrollment.
Security, validation, and permission checks for student enrollment are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important student enrollment behaviors.
